title: lsonitrosoacetylacetone and lsonitrosoacetophenone as Extractants and Spectrophotometric Reagents for lron(ll)
author: U. B. TALWAR
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Inorganic and Nuclear Chemistry Laboratory, Institute of Science, Bombay-32 

 ( Received  14  September , 1970) 

 HINAA and HINAP have been employed for rapid separation and spectrophotometric determination of iron(ll) by extraction into butanol and chloroform respectively. As an extractant and spectrophotometric reagent for Fe(ll), HINAP is better than HINAA. The species extracted into butanol and chloroform are Fe(INAA) 2  and Fe(INAP) 2  respectively. The  p K values of the extraction equilibrium constants at 29° are 2.9±0.2 and 3.1±0.3 for the systems Fe(II)-Na 2 S 2 O 3  -HINAA-butanol and Fe(ll)-Na 2 S 2 O 3  -HINAP chloroform respectively.
steps_raw:

As an extractant and spectrophotometric reagent for Fe(ll), HINAP is better than HINAA. The species extracted into butanol and chloroform are Fe(INAA) 2  and Fe(INAP) 2  respectively. The  p K values of the extraction equilibrium constants at 29° are 2.9±0.2 and 3.1±0.3 for the systems Fe(II)-Na 2 S 2 O 3  -HINAA-butanol and Fe(ll)-Na 2 S 2 O 3  -HINAP chloroform respectively.
